predecessor.lost_artifacts: All training progress in /workspace/pact/lane_q_faithful_results/ — no archive harvested

redeployed_to.label: q_faithful_a100_sxm4_80g_redeploy
redeployed_to.fallback_reason: H100 SXM 34383502 (instance 35985637) created with success=true but Vast.ai queued it as 'Required resources currently unavailable' for 12+min — provisioning failed. Per mission spec FALLBACK to A100 SXM4 80GB. The Czechia A100 has 99.9% reliability and is RUNNING (not queued). Saves $1.40/hr vs H100 with comparable wallclock (A100 SXM4 80GB is ~2.5x speedup vs RTX 4090 instead of H100's ~5x — still saves ~7-9h of wallclock vs 4090).

torch_pin_decision.rationale: A100 driver 560.35.03 < 580 → cu124 wheel REQUIRED per CLAUDE.md driver-pin rule (`feedback_vast_cuda_driver_too_old_silent_cpu_fallback_20260501.md`). Bootstrap function in scripts/remote_archive_only_eval.sh auto-detects driver_major < 580 and exports the cu124 spec automatically — no operator override needed.

bootstrap_fixes_required: apt-get install -y unzip (missing from pytorch image)
Verify torch.cuda.is_available() == True BEFORE training (CLAUDE.md non-negotiable)
harvest_trigger: When train.log shows 'LANE_Q_FAITHFUL_DONE' OR auth_eval.log appears, scp archive_lane_q_faithful.zip + auth_eval.log + eval_work/result.json
council_verdict: 7/7 ENDORSE H100 swap (Shannon: wallclock-bound on Phase 2 KL distill; Dykstra: feasibility region intersection unaffected by chip; Yousfi: KL distill T=2.0 unchanged; Fridrich: arch unchanged; Contrarian: 4090 preemption FORCES new chip choice; Quantizr: H100 SXM is what real labs use; Hotz: 13h saved at $5 incremental cost is the ROI no-brainer)
